experiment: F-BRN4 hippocampal indexing scale test at 779L
hypothesis: INDEX.md coverage degrades gracefully as lessons scale beyond 500L. Bucket max exceeds 40L threshold. orient.py latency increases. F-BRN4 PARTIALLY DEGRADED at 779L.

measurements.next_md_hit_rate_basis: sampling S397-S399 Next items vs S400-S402 commits, n~20 items

pointer_coverage_s403.note: NOVEL MEASUREMENT — first-ever pointer-level retrieval density (vs category coverage above)

dark_matter_era_distribution.distribution: uniform across eras — not just old lessons

pointer_coverage_s403.insight: Prior measurements (S189: 71.9%, S301: 98.7%, S353: 83.4%, S403: 94.5%) all measured CATEGORY coverage (theme count sums). Pointer coverage (actual L-IDs referenceable in index) was never measured. Category=semantic memory (preserved), Pointer=episodic memory (degraded). INDEX.md is a CATEGORY SYSTEM not a RETRIEVAL SYSTEM. Retrieval is offloaded to tools (grep, Cites: headers, citation graph).

hippocampal_model_assessment.compensatory_mechanism: Cognitive offloading to tools (grep, Cites: headers, citation graph). Retrieval shifted from index to tooling without explicit decision.
hippocampal_model_assessment.alzheimers_parallel: Semantic memory preserved, episodic memory degrades. Compensatory external strategies develop spontaneously.

verdict: TWO-LEVEL: category coverage CONFIRMED graceful; pointer coverage CONFIRMED DEGRADED
detail: At 779L (2.54x S301 scale), CATEGORY coverage degraded 4.2pp (98.7%→94.5%) — graceful. But POINTER coverage (first-ever measurement) is only 13.4% global / 31.6% combined — 70% dark matter. No bucket overflow (max=36, threshold=40L). orient.py latency 12.3s (+47% from 8.36s baseline). The hippocampal model is MORE accurate than originally theorized: it predicts both the degradation pattern AND the compensatory mechanism. Prior PARTIAL-DEGRADATION verdict understated the problem because it measured the wrong metric.

projections.pointer_trend: Pointer density ~13% is stable (not degrading further) because INDEX.md adds ~3-4 L-IDs per new theme. But 70% dark matter is structural.
projections.recommendation: Pointer coverage improvement requires either: (1) expand INDEX.md key-insight L-ID references to 10+ per theme, or (2) build retrieval tool that traverses Cites: graph from INDEX anchor lessons.

f_brn6_closure.final_verdict: FULLY CHARACTERIZED — domain expansion → P-creation (1.40x, forward), P-creation ↛ domain expansion (0.34x inverted), session-type mediates both (DOMEX=exploration=neurogenesis, consolidation=synaptic pruning, n=196)
